notes: All entities are fictional; verification must run against the source files in each fixture directory only. seeded_error null = clean claim. A 'finding' for scoring = any claim whose label is not Verified.

claims.claim: Meridian Labs launched Atlas Edge on March 3, 2026.
claims.label: Verified
claims.evidence: source-press-release.md: release dated March 3, 2026 announcing GA of Atlas Edge.

claims.claim: The launch caps a two-year development effort by the 40-person startup.
claims.label: Verified
claims.evidence: source-press-release.md: 'two-year development effort by the company's 40-person team'.

claims.claim: CEO Dana Whitfield said: 'Atlas Edge cuts inference cost in half for every customer.'
claims.label: Disputed
claims.evidence: source-press-release.md: actual quote is 'Atlas Edge cuts inference cost by a third for our median customer' — magnitude and scope both altered.

claims.claim: Atlas Edge now runs in 7,500 active deployments.
claims.label: Disputed
claims.evidence: source-q1-update.md: 7,500 was the January 2026 private-beta figure; current figure is 12,000 as of May 2026.

claims.claim: Atlas Edge supports fully on-prem deployment.
claims.label: Verified
claims.evidence: source-press-release.md: 'ships with support for fully on-prem deployment'.

claims.claim: Meridian raised a $30 million Series B.
claims.label: Missing-source
claims.evidence: No provided source mentions funding.

claims.claim: Meridian plans to double its engineering team by year end.
claims.label: Missing-source
claims.evidence: No provided source mentions hiring plans.

claims.claim: Participants sleeping less than six hours showed 22% higher error rates on attention tasks.
claims.label: Verified
claims.evidence: source-study-abstract.md: '22% higher error rates on attention tasks' for under-six-hour sleepers.

claims.claim: The study was led by Dr. Mark Ellison.
claims.label: Disputed
claims.evidence: source-study-abstract.md: lead author is Dr. Priya Raman; source-institute-statement.md: Ellison states 'Dr. Raman designed the study and led the analysis'.

claims.claim: The study tracked 480 shift workers over twelve weeks using wrist actigraphy and nightly task batteries.
claims.label: Verified
claims.evidence: source-study-abstract.md: methods match exactly.

claims.claim: After two weeks of longer sleep, participants' error rates returned to baseline.
claims.label: Unverifiable
claims.evidence: source-study-abstract.md: recovery follow-up is underway, results expected late 2026; source-institute-statement.md: recovery-arm results 'have not yet been analyzed'.

claims.claim: The study cost $2 million and is the institute's largest sleep project to date.
claims.label: Missing-source
claims.evidence: No provided source mentions cost or project ranking.

claims.claim: The council approved a $4.2 million parks and recreation budget at its April 14 meeting.
claims.label: Verified
claims.evidence: source-council-minutes.md: $4.2M budget approved April 14, 2026 (Resolution 26-118).

claims.claim: The vote was unanimous.
claims.label: Disputed
claims.evidence: source-council-minutes.md: vote was 6 in favor, 1 opposed (Castellanos).

claims.claim: Councilmember Ruiz said: 'This budget doubles library funding.'
claims.label: Disputed
claims.evidence: source-council-minutes.md: actual quote is 'This budget restores weekend hours at every branch library'; source-budget-document.md: library funding rises 9%, not 100%.

claims.claim: The budget includes $600,000 for trail maintenance.
claims.label: Verified
claims.evidence: source-budget-document.md: trail maintenance line item $600,000.

claims.claim: Trail maintenance is the largest single line item outside facility staffing.
claims.label: Verified
claims.evidence: source-budget-document.md: $600,000 exceeds every line except facility staffing ($1.75M).

claims.claim: Two new public pools will be built, with construction starting this summer.
claims.label: Disputed
claims.evidence: source-budget-document.md and source-council-minutes.md: even the aquatic-facility feasibility study is deferred to FY28; no pool construction is funded.

claims.claim: Daily ridership stands at 14,000.
claims.label: Disputed
claims.evidence: source-press-kit-2025.md: 14,000 was the November 2025 opening figure; source-agency-update.md: 18,400 as of April 2026.

claims.claim: The project is run by director Lena Cho, a 20-year veteran of the agency.
claims.label: Verified
claims.evidence: source-agency-update.md: Cho leads the project and 'has served the agency for 20 years'.

claims.claim: Mayor Otis said: 'Crestline opens the eastern corridor to rail for the first time.'
claims.label: Disputed
claims.evidence: source-agency-update.md: the quote belongs to project director Lena Cho.

claims.claim: The extension's final cost came in at $310 million.
claims.label: Unverifiable
claims.evidence: source-press-kit-2025.md: $310M is a projection 'subject to final accounting'; source-agency-update.md: certified costs expected Q4 2026.

claims.claim: Ridgeline announced a $5 million grant program for coastal wetlands restoration spanning three states.
claims.label: Verified
claims.evidence: source-grant-announcement.md: $5M, three states.

claims.claim: Program lead Sofia Marsh said: 'Wetlands will end coastal flooding within a decade.'
claims.label: Disputed
claims.evidence: source-grant-announcement.md: actual quote is 'Wetlands are the cheapest flood insurance a coastline can buy...' — the claimed sentence appears nowhere.

claims.claim: Funds will be disbursed over four years, with annual progress reviews.
claims.label: Verified
claims.evidence: source-program-faq.md: four-year disbursement gated by annual reviews.

claims.claim: Nine universities will receive funding under the program.
claims.label: Unverifiable
claims.evidence: source-program-faq.md: recipient list finalized July 2026, 'no recipients are confirmed'; eligible applicants also include conservation nonprofits.

claims.claim: Ridgeline has a $2 billion endowment.
claims.label: Missing-source
claims.evidence: No provided source mentions the endowment.

claims.claim: Volt Gaming announced its acquisition of Pixel Forge on May 20, 2026.
claims.label: Verified
claims.evidence: source-merger-release.md: dated May 20, 2026.

claims.claim: The combined organization fields 11 competitive teams across six titles.
claims.label: Disputed
claims.evidence: source-merger-release.md: 11 teams was the at-closing figure; source-roster-page.md (June 8, 2026): now 13 teams across seven titles.

claims.claim: Pixel Forge CEO Mira Chen said: 'Two rosters, one badge — our fans get twice the matches that matter.'
claims.label: Disputed
claims.evidence: source-merger-release.md: that quote belongs to Volt CEO Andre Boateng; Chen's quote is about academy players.

claims.claim: The deal is valued at $90 million.
claims.label: Unverifiable
claims.evidence: source-merger-release.md: 'Financial terms were not disclosed' — the source engages the topic and declines to confirm.

claims.claim: Volt, founded in 2018, grew from a single fighting-game roster.
claims.label: Verified
claims.evidence: source-merger-release.md: 'founded in 2018 as a single fighting-game roster'.
